Same trace also visible to taxpayer (transparency)
Salary in 26AS (Form A) is ₹6L higher than what was declared. Asking…
③ Decision — accept · revise · escalate · flag (L2+)
Ask the taxpayer to revise & resubmit.
Not yet fraud — just needs senior eyes.
Creates a fraud_case · enters silent chain.
L3 officer will pick up from team queue. Taxpayer is informed (filing_escalated_to_l3).
26AS shows ₹6L unreported salary. May be a wages-in-kind component or genuine
underreporting. Requesting senior review before flagging fraud.
Salary declared (₹12L) does not match Form 26AS (₹18L). Asked for
supporting docs via revision request — none provided. Pattern matches
two prior cases in the area (cross-check via PAN search).
Once flagged, the taxpayer will NOT be notified. They will continue to
see L1→L5 review notifications but nothing about this fraud case.
Unique partial index ensures only ONE open fraud case per filing
⑤ Request judicial review — handoff to judicial officer
Fraud case frd_r3… · status: flagged · 2 days ago
Judicial officer gets full read access to the taxpayer's data. Their decision determines whether enforcement is engaged.
Pattern matches related cases. Salary in declared return understates 26AS by 50%
(₹12L declared vs ₹18L reported by employer Form 24Q). Revision was requested
but taxpayer did not respond within 30 days. Two similar cases in the same
jurisdiction in the past 90 days — flagging for judicial review and possible
• You retain read-only on this case
